summary: Moved several SysDolphin BaseLib FObj routines closer to C/decompiled coverage. The PR replaced assembly for HSD_FObjStopAnim and HSD_FObjStopAnimAll with C implementations, added a C-side NON_MATCHING version of HSD_FObjReqAnimAll while keeping an inline asm fallback for exact behavior, and renamed known assembly symbols such as func_8036AC10 to parseFloat and func_8036B030 to HSD_FObjInterpretAnim. It also updated FObj prototypes to use more descriptive parameter names like startframe, update_func, and rate.
what_changed: Removed large assembly bodies for HSD_FObjReqAnimAll, func_8036AB24, and HSD_FObjStopAnimAll from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/fobj.s, leaving later routines and renamed labels in assembly.
Added a NON_MATCHING C implementation of HSD_FObjReqAnim and HSD_FObjReqAnimAll in src/sysdolphin/baselib/fobj.c, guarded with an inline asm implementation when NON_MATCHING is not enabled.
Implemented HSD_FObjStopAnim in C, including the HSD_A_OP_KEY check and call to HSD_FObjInterpretAnim before clearing the low state bits in fobj->flags.
Implemented C inline helpers FObj_FlushKeyData and HSD_FObjStopAnim_inline, then used them in HSD_FObjStopAnimAll to iterate through the FObj linked list.
Renamed func_8036AB24 references to HSD_FObjStopAnim, including a callsite in asm/melee/text_3.s.
Renamed func_8036AC10 to parseFloat in assembly labels and callsites.
Renamed func_8036B030 to HSD_FObjInterpretAnim in assembly labels and callsites.
Updated function declarations in src/sysdolphin/baselib/fobj.h to use descriptive argument names: startframe, update_func, and rate.
Updated include/functions.h comment stubs to reflect new known names HSD_FObjStopAnim, parseFloat, and HSD_FObjInterpretAnim.
smart_moves: Kept HSD_FObjReqAnimAll exact via inline asm while documenting a nearby NON_MATCHING C version, preserving build/matching status while making the inferred logic searchable and reviewable.
Used #pragma push, #pragma peephole on, and #pragma pop around the C implementations of the stop-animation routines, suggesting compiler-codegen control was important for matching these functions.
Factored the repeated key-flush behavior into inline helpers so HSD_FObjStopAnim and HSD_FObjStopAnimAll could share logic while still allowing inlining-sensitive codegen.
Renamed function symbols in assembly at the same time as adding C implementations, improving cross-file callsite readability without needing all surrounding functions to be decompiled.
Preserved exact low-nibble flag manipulation in C as `(0 & 0xF) | (fobj->flags & 0xF0)` and `(1 & 0xF) | (fobj->flags & 0xF0)`, mirroring the assembly's state-field semantics.
decomp_lessons: For FObj matching, signed integer to float conversion can be the blocker: the added comment says HSD_FObjReqAnimAll is non-matching because of the conversion to float, and the asm uses the common PowerPC xoris/lis 0x4330/lfd/fsubs pattern.

When a list-processing routine is hard to match wholesale, it can be useful to write a clear NON_MATCHING C version and retain an asm fallback until the exact conversion or scheduling issue is solved.

Small state setters on packed flag fields should preserve unrelated bits explicitly; this PR consistently treats the low nibble of fobj->flags as the animation state and preserves the high nibble with `& 0xF0`.
Inline helper functions can be used to influence whether a list-wide `All` routine matches the original code layout, especially when single-object and list-object variants share behavior.

Known library names should be propagated into asm callsites and headers as soon as evidence is strong enough; this makes later decompilation work easier even if dependent functions remain in assembly.
naming_conventions: HSD_FObjReqAnimAll uses parameter name `startframe` rather than generic `frame`.
HSD_FObjStopAnim and HSD_FObjStopAnimAll use `update_func` or `obj_update` for callback-like update parameters.
Animation rate parameters are named `rate` rather than `frame` in stop/interpret functions.
Internal helper names use mixed BaseLib style, including `FObj_FlushKeyData` and `HSD_FObjStopAnim_inline`.
Resolved anonymous function labels to library-style names: `func_8036AB24` -> `HSD_FObjStopAnim`, `func_8036AC10` -> `parseFloat`, and `func_8036B030` -> `HSD_FObjInterpretAnim`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#ifdef NON_MATCHING` to keep a readable C version of HSD_FObjReqAnimAll while compiling the exact inline assembly path by default.
Declared small-data constants `extern const f32 lbl_804DE4D0;` and `extern const f64 lbl_804DE4D8;` for the inline asm float conversion sequence.
The exact asm for HSD_FObjReqAnimAll uses the PowerPC signed-int-to-double conversion idiom with `xoris`, stack stores, `lis r5, 0x4330`, `lfd`, and subtracting the magic double constant.
Applied `#pragma peephole on` around newly decompiled stop-animation routines, indicating peephole optimization affected matching.
Used inline functions for FObj stop-all behavior to reproduce likely original inlining and avoid introducing extra calls.
Left still-unmatched or not-yet-decompiled routines such as parseFloat and HSD_FObjInterpretAnim in assembly but renamed their labels and branch targets.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match HSD_FObjReqAnimAll or remove the inline asm fallback.
Search for additional callsites updated from func_8036AB24 to HSD_FObjStopAnim.
Compare later definitions of parseFloat to confirm the name and signature.
Review FObj flag/state handling in later PRs for confirmed enum names for the low-nibble states.
Look for follow-up changes to HSD_FObjInterpretAnim signature and callback parameter naming.
